figures.caption: Figure 1: One HDET fan-out/converge cycle with N=4 replicas and spread ratio α. All replicas share the same all-reduced gradient at each step but take steps of different sizes, causing parameters to diverge. Every T steps an AllReduce average collapses the ensemble into a single model. Learning rates are randomly reassigned (reshuffled) before the next cycle.

Table 1: Training loss on production news-feed data. Lower is better. † indicates training crash (loss grew unboundedly). All HDET variants use ηmax=0.0009, which is 9× higher than the conservative baseline.

Figure 3: Per-group LR schedules for four parameter groups. Solid: baseline (OneCycleLR, ηmax=0.0009). Dashed: HDET GPU 0 (lowest-LR replica, ρ0·0.0009=0.0008); the 8-replica mean equals the 0.0009 baseline. Periodic fluctuations mark each sync interval where the auto-LR controller updates the base schedule.

Figure 2: Training loss vs. steps at ηmax=0.0009. Baseline (gray) crashes at ≈18K steps, stabilizing at 4.169. Warm-Init (teal) survives to ≈185K steps then diverges (4.674): warm initialization extends stability but cannot replace weight averaging. HDET (dark blue) converges smoothly to 3.277—below Baseline-Low (3.294, not shown) trained at 9× lower LR.
